---
title: USCIS Immigration / Citizenship Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: uscis-immigration-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# USCIS Immigration / Citizenship Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The USCIS Immigration / Citizenship Attribute Service Adapter is the **immigration-status attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes attribute-disclosure events from USCIS — the federal authority of record for immigration and citizenship status — and emits canonical claims with the disclosure-audit envelope.

The primary integration surface is the **SAVE (Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements) program** for benefits-eligibility verification. Disclosures are subject to **Privacy Act SORN DHS/USCIS-004 routine uses**; every entitlement must cite the applicable SAVE-program routine use in addition to the consuming engagement's authority.

Three primary functions:

1. **SAVE-program disclosure observation.** Watches SAVE responses as they are issued at consuming benefits-eligibility decision points, emitting canonical claims with the disclosed immigration status, A-Number reference (when permitted), and benefits-eligibility result.
2. **Citizenship-verification observation.** Captures citizenship-verification disclosures for engagements that require it (federal employment eligibility, certain federal benefits), emitting canonical claims with the canonical authority citation.
3. **Routine-use entitlement enforcement.** For each disclosure, validates that the consuming engagement has the applicable Privacy Act SORN routine use cited in its entitlement; engagements without the citation are rejected before any USCIS API call.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `immigration-status`, `citizenship-verification`, `save-program`

**Reads:** USCIS SAVE-program API and the documented citizenship-verification channels. Access gated by the Privacy Act SORN DHS/USCIS-004 routine-use citation and the consuming-engagement entitlement; both recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`.

**Emits:** `uscis-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim with immigration status, A-Number reference when permitted, citizenship determination), `uscis-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with entitlement reference, Privacy Act SORN routine-use citation, USCIS disclosure id), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** initiate immigration adjudication, modify immigration records, access case-file detail beyond what the disclosure surfaces, or store A-Number values outside the documented retention and encryption envelope.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | Immigration / citizenship claims supplement identification when work-eligibility or citizen-only access is required. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | USCIS disclosure id is the stable identifier per disclosure event. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope, entitlement reference, SORN routine-use citation. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All USCIS channels are TLS-protected. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Validation uses FIPS-validated cryptographic libraries. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

USCIS is the federal authority of record for immigration and citizenship status. Primary integration surface is the SAVE (Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements) program for benefits-eligibility verification. Subject to Privacy Act SORN DHS/USCIS-004 routine uses. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family — pairs with [`dhs-everify-attribute-service`](../dhs-everify-attribute-service/dhs-everify-attribute-service.html) (E-Verify composite authority includes USCIS) and [`ssa-attribute-service`](../ssa-attribute-service/ssa-attribute-service.html) (cross-correlation in I-9 / benefits flows).

ZTMM pillars: identity, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
